jokes.text: You never see people drinking grape juice. It's delicious, you'd love it. I think it's a very underrated juice.
jokes.setup: Casual morning conversation about grape juice as antioxidant
jokes.punchline: Extended philosophical discussion about grape juice's place in society
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David minutiae - turning mundane juice into deep sociological observation. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Yeah, he's a bald guy, too. It's very bad for the bald community.
jokes.setup: Discussion of sex offender in neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Larry's concern is primarily for the reputation of bald people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David logic - making everything about himself while missing the actual point. Excellent character integration and economy.

jokes.text: I know, as long as he's gonna be a sex offender I wish he had a full head of hair.
jokes.setup: Previous joke about bald community suffering
jokes.punchline: Larry doubles down with completely backwards priorities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that makes the previous joke even funnier. Dark humor with perfect Larry David twisted logic.

jokes.text: A guy in a suit is taking my paper? A suit's a good cover if a guy's gonna steal a paper.
jokes.setup: Well-dressed man seen taking Larry's newspaper
jokes.punchline: Larry's theory about formal wear as criminal disguise
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about criminal psychology and class assumptions.

jokes.text: Guy in a suit's gotta be able to afford a paper.
jokes.setup: Continued bewilderment about newspaper theft
jokes.punchline: Logical assumption about wealth and petty crime
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for the mystery that will drive the episode. Good logical progression.

jokes.text: I've got the touch. Watch watch watch watch. I just know how to do it. I know this machine.
jokes.setup: Larry claims expertise with finicky vending machine
jokes.punchline: Overconfident demonstration of dubious skill
jokes.explanation: Classic male overconfidence with mechanical objects. Visual comedy element assumed.

jokes.text: I'm not an artist, you know?
jokes.setup: Fan asking about creative process behind Seinfeld episodes
jokes.punchline: Larry's modest deflection of artistic pretensions
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor that feels authentic to Larry's persona.

jokes.text: Probably the biggest 'Seinfeld' fan ever... 'Puffy shirt' is my favorite episode.
jokes.setup: Rick claims to be biggest Seinfeld fan
jokes.punchline: References episode that's actually from the show Larry created
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Larry's real career - Rick unknowingly praising Larry's work to Larry

jokes.text: Oh, boy, look at the jew girl. I am so jewish right now.
jokes.setup: Cheryl preparing traditional Jewish food
jokes.punchline: Non-Jewish wife's enthusiastic cultural immersion
jokes.explanation: Charming fish-out-of-water comedy with Cheryl embracing Jewish traditions.

jokes.text: I wish my mother were alive to hear you say that word out loud.
jokes.setup: Cheryl correctly pronouncing 'haroset'
jokes.punchline: Larry's morbid reference to his dead mother's hypothetical pride
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's both touching and morbid - very Larry David.

jokes.text: 'You people' I dunno if I like that so much. You jews, is that better? That's better, yeah.
jokes.setup: Larry says 'you people' about Jewish customs
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's correction leads to even more direct phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment about political correctness and directness.

jokes.text: I'll bet you Mark is the guy who's taking the paper.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes Mark moved back in around when papers started disappearing
jokes.punchline: Larry's conspiracy theory connects the dots
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot connection and escalation. Larry's paranoid logic at its finest.

jokes.text: Yeah, but not if he's upset with somebody who promised him a gift for Christmas and never followed through on it.
jokes.setup: Cheryl points out Mark could afford a newspaper
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals his guilt about the unfulfilled Christmas gift
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation that adds depth to the paranoia while being funny.

jokes.text: You don't understand because you're not jewish, okay? But it's a jewish thing. Invite the cranky neighbors who... well, if jews don't have any place to go for seder, you're supposed to invite them.
jokes.setup: Larry wants to invite Mac and Ethel to seder
jokes.punchline: Larry's made-up Jewish tradition about inviting cranky neighbors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant improvised tradition that sounds plausible while being completely self-serving.

jokes.text: Yeah, and I eat and I breathe. In that order.
jokes.setup: Asked if he plays golf
jokes.punchline: Rick's priorities hierarchy with golf as #1
jokes.explanation: Good delivery on a golf obsession joke, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: I'll bring latkes.
jokes.setup: Rick invited to Passover seder
jokes.punchline: Rick offers to bring the wrong holiday food
jokes.explanation: Good cultural confusion moment - latkes are Hanukkah, not Passover.

jokes.text: Oh, and I forgot to mention it's that sex offender.
jokes.setup: Larry tells Cheryl about inviting his new friend
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of the friend's criminal status
jokes.explanation: Masterful delayed revelation that completely recontextualizes the previous conversation. Perfect timing and economy.

jokes.text: You invited a sex offender over for seder? Are you out of your mind?
jokes.setup: Larry's casual revelation about Rick
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's completely reasonable horror
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that grounds the absurdity.

jokes.text: What would Jesus do? Cheryl, Cheryl. Cheryl, he's a jew. He's a jew!
jokes.setup: Cheryl invokes WWJD about the sex offender
jokes.punchline: Larry's logic that Jewish solidarity trumps criminal behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twisted logic that perfectly captures Larry's priorities and moral confusion.

jokes.text: Washington and then you go 77 years, Lincoln. 77 years there's... what's that guy's name? Theodore Roosevelt? F.D.R. And then another 77 years, you got W.
jokes.setup: Jake explains his theory of great presidents every 77 years
jokes.punchline: The theory culminates with George W. Bush as a 'great' president
jokes.explanation: Good political satire through pseudo-intellectual conservative theory.

jokes.text: You couldn't cut it in your single life there? Couldn't get any dates, so you went back?
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on Mark's reconciliation with Marla
jokes.punchline: Larry's tactless assumption about Mark's dating failures
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social inappropriateness and lack of filter.

jokes.text: You ever get aroused?
jokes.setup: Larry asking Mark about being a cosmetic surgeon
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate personal question
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriate curiosity delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: Have you been stealing my newspaper?
jokes.setup: Larry working up to ask Mark directly
jokes.punchline: Blunt accusation after building suspense
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the building paranoia. Larry's directness creates maximum awkwardness.

jokes.text: Okay, so that's two inappropriate questions.
jokes.setup: Mark calls the newspaper question inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual accounting of his social transgressions
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Larry's complete lack of social awareness while keeping score.

jokes.text: Somebody order latkes?
jokes.setup: Rick arriving at the seder with his promised contribution
jokes.punchline: Cheerful delivery of wrong holiday food
jokes.explanation: Good callback execution though the setup was telegraphed.

jokes.text: This guy completely changed my life. I'm telling you. I can't believe the way I hit the ball now on account of him.
jokes.setup: Larry introduces Rick at religious gathering
jokes.punchline: Gushing praise for golf instructor who's also sex offender
jokes.explanation: Dark irony - Larry's genuine enthusiasm for someone everyone else fears

jokes.text: Somebody drop a cute bomb in here?
jokes.setup: Rick meeting Sammy, Jeff's young daughter
jokes.punchline: Rick's inappropriately flirtatious comment about a child
jokes.explanation: Deeply uncomfortable moment that plays on the audience's knowledge of Rick's status.

jokes.text: What the hell are you trying to pull, Larry? He had nowhere to go, what do you want me to do? I don't give a shit where he goes.
jokes.setup: Jeff confronts Larry about bringing Rick
jokes.punchline: Larry's weak defense met with Jeff's brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of tension and Jeff's reasonable outrage.

jokes.text: Jeff's horror reaction when he realizes who Rick is
jokes.setup: Jeff recognizing Rick as the sex offender
jokes.punchline: His visible panic and immediate confrontation with Larry
jokes.explanation: Good reaction comedy - Jeff's horror at the situation Larry has created

jokes.text: Take a look around this room here, okay, and just see if there's anybody who you recognize.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Ethel to look around the room
jokes.punchline: Larry's setup to expose the newspaper thief
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the upcoming revelation, building suspense effectively.

jokes.text: Okay, gently. Gently, guys. Let's put her here on the bed.
jokes.setup: Ethel has fainted after seeing Rick
jokes.punchline: Medical emergency interrupts Larry's detective work
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that escalates the situation and provides plot complications.

jokes.text: A couple of hours?! What, are you kidding? Just a... you know, she'll sleep it off, then she'll be fine.
jokes.setup: Mark explains Ethel will be unconscious for hours
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismay at losing his witness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's frustration as his plan falls apart.

jokes.text: You know what's in here? Matzah. And I'm gonna hide this, and you've got to go find it and whoever finds the matzah is gonna get a dollar.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the afikoman tradition to kids
jokes.punchline: Larry's enthusiastic but simplified explanation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the later cheating revelation, but mild on its own.

jokes.text: I mean, a really good look. If she ever sees him again, she'll recognize him. She'll know that face. She never forgets a face.
jokes.setup: Larry talking about his wife recognizing the newspaper thief
jokes.punchline: Larry unknowingly describing Cheryl's ability to identify Mark
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony as Larry unknowingly threatens Mark while he sits there.

jokes.text: Excuse me, uh, no. Just some gefilte fish.
jokes.setup: Asked if something's wrong after Larry's threat
jokes.punchline: Mark's nervous deflection
jokes.explanation: Good nervous reaction showing Mark's guilt and discomfort.

jokes.text: Oh, my foot fell asleep so I was kind of jumping up and down to get the blood circulating again. I hate when that happens, right?
jokes.setup: Loud noises heard from upstairs where Ethel is
jokes.punchline: Mark's completely implausible excuse
jokes.explanation: Weak excuse that obviously covers something suspicious upstairs.

jokes.text: I've been paged by the hospital. It must be an emergency. I didn't hear anything, doctor. I keep my pager on vibrate... for medical emergencies, yes.
jokes.setup: Mark claims he's been paged for emergency
jokes.punchline: Larry's pointed observation about not hearing any pager
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as Larry catches Mark in increasingly obvious lies.

jokes.text: Aren't you a cosmetic surgeon? Yes, we have emergencies in that field as well.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning the urgency of cosmetic surgery
jokes.punchline: Mark's defensive insistence on cosmetic emergencies
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier profession discussion with increasing pressure.

jokes.text: Emergency boob job, no doubt.
jokes.setup: Mark insisting on cosmetic surgery emergency
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic dismissal
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic escalation that shows Larry's complete disbelief.

jokes.text: You're lucky that old lady fainted.
jokes.setup: Mark trying to leave
jokes.punchline: Larry's direct accusation about Mark's convenient timing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant revelation moment where Larry connects all the dots publicly.

jokes.text: You took that paper. I know you did. I'm a doctor, you idiot! Good yontif.
jokes.setup: Final confrontation between Larry and Mark
jokes.punchline: Mark's angry denial and ironic holiday blessing
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax with the ironic Jewish holiday greeting after calling Larry an idiot.

jokes.text: Well, I saw Mr. Dunkle whisper in his son's ear and then the boy went right over and found the matzah.
jokes.setup: Rick's moral quandary about telling Larry something
jokes.punchline: Rick reveals he witnessed the cheating at the afikoman game
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup, though the revelation itself is relatively minor.

jokes.text: My friend told me, that's how I know! He saw you whisper in your son's ear and then watched as he ran to the bookcase and got the matzah.
jokes.setup: Jake demanding to know how Larry knows about the cheating
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals his source is Rick
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that sets up the final revelation about Rick.

jokes.text: Oh, your friend the convicted sex offender!
jokes.setup: Larry citing Rick as his witness
jokes.punchline: Jake's explosive revelation of Rick's true identity
jokes.explanation: Explosive climax where the sex offender secret is finally revealed to everyone. Perfect dramatic timing.

jokes.text: I do.
jokes.setup: Request for someone who knows mouth-to-mouth
jokes.punchline: The sex offender volunteers for mouth-to-mouth with a child
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect dark comedy climax. Two simple words create maximum horror and impossible moral choice. Peak Curb moment.
